item.title: Is Iran Winning?
item.description: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains why Iran believes it has the upper hand.

item.title: ‘Stop This Madness’: Time to Rein In Trump’s Reckless Conduct
item.description: Readers react to President Trump’s increasingly bellicose rhetoric. Also: Two moon missions, 58 years apart.

item.title: It’s Called Silicon Sampling, and It’s Going to Ruin Public Opinion Polling
item.description: Instead of navigating the obstacles to conduct polls with human respondents, pollsters are running A.I. simulations instead. Why?

item.title: Didn’t We Already Win the Race to the Moon?
item.description: Should we just send robots? An astronaut, a scientist and a journalist debate.

item.title: Why Iranians Believe They Have the Upper Hand
item.description: Iran believes time is on its side, and that the West isn’t ready for the economic shock of a blocked Strait of Hormuz, warns Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution.

item.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz Is Iran’s Trump Card
item.description: On “The Opinions,” the columnist David French says that no matter how much havoc Trump wreaks on Iranian targets, as long as they maintain control of the Strait of Hormuz, Iran can keep going.

item.title: These Wall Street Investors Are Starting to Panic
item.description: The cockroaches are starting to emerge.

item.title: San Francisco Sobers Up
item.description: A city gets its act together.

item.title: I’ve Covered Women in the Workplace for 15 Years. Something Alarming Is Happening.
item.description: As women are erased from the narrative, injustices against them go unnoticed.

item.title: The Old Way of Campaigning Won’t Cut It Anymore
item.description: Is this a great way to audition and select our leaders, especially for executive offices? Not particularly.

item.title: The Light That Changed My Life
item.description: Just one candle in the darkness.

item.title: The War Is Turning Iran Into a Major World Power
item.description: Its newfound might derives from its control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Teaching in an American University Feels Very Strange Right Now
item.description: We’re supposed to give students a map. I don’t even know the terrain.

item.title: Enough With the Nepo Candidates, Democrats
item.description: Democrats smother their best message — change — when they try to reserve public offices for relatives and cronies.

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead? A Debate.
item.description: A ‘Christian Atheist’ joins Ross Douthat.

item.title: Change Doesn’t Happen the Way You Think
item.description: The self-transformation industry sells control. Real change is messier.

item.title: Easter Reminds Us That Resurrection Is Everywhere
item.description: Marriages, careers, reputations, financial stability and dreams can all die. But that’s not the end of the story.

item.title: What Trump Is Doing to the English Language
item.description: There is a morality to verbs, especially in political speech.

item.title: ‘An Operational Success and a Huge Strategic Failure’
item.description: As one expert puts it, this war is “an operational success but a huge strategic failure.”

item.title: Finish the Job: How Trump Can Still Win in Iran
item.description: Our strength and leverage over events in Iran are growing, not diminishing.

item.title: I Was Broadway’s First Grizabella. I Couldn’t Have Imagined the New ‘Cats.’
item.description: A new, queer reimagining of “Cats” showed me something about the show I never expected.

item.title: Not Just for Rock Stars: Playing in the Band
item.description: Readers respond to “Change Your Life. Start a Band,” an Opinion guest essay by Hugo Lindgren. Also: Bruce Springsteen’s art.

item.title: Iran Is Ending the Dream of Remote-Controlled War
item.description: A.I.’s significant targeting improvements aren’t enough to overcome geography in Iran.

item.title: Hormones Don’t Need to Make You Live Longer to Be Worth It
item.description: Estrogen patches are in scarce supply because of increased demand — that’s mostly a good thing.

item.title: Trump Killed the One Thing That Made Filing Taxes Easier
item.description: Tax preparation companies and Republicans are pursuing their shared interest in torturing taxpayers.

item.title: Pam and Kristi, Kicked to the Curb
item.description: Trump girls gone wild — or just gone.

item.title: All the President’s Wars — at Home and Abroad
item.description: From Tehran to the Supreme Court, a look at Trump’s relentless battles.

item.title: Trump’s Relentless, ‘Utterly Incoherent’ Battles
item.description: A look at a conflict without end and a constitution under pressure.

item.title: Memories of the ‘Last, Best Time to Be a Kid’
item.description: Readers react to Sarah Wildman’s guest essay about the free-range childhood depicted in the Rob Reiner film “Stand by Me.”

item.title: Trump Needs Smarter Sycophants
item.description: Loyal losers don’t go very far with this president.

item.title: What if Iran Wins This War?
item.description: If Iran wins this war, victory “will be tremendously emboldening for a regime that has been very dangerous even at its weaker moments,” Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution, argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: What if Trump Just Walked Away From Iran?
item.description: Iran will become “the toll collector at the gulf” if Trump preemptively walks away from the conflict in Iran, Suzanne Maloney, the director of foreign policy at the Brookings Institution, argues on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Our First Moon Colony Will Be Filled With Garbage
item.description: Forget futuristic glass domes filled with plants and high-tech infrastructure, Jared Isaacman, the head of NASA, says of humanity’s first space colonies. In reality, because of the cost of travel, they will most likely be scrappy bases surrounded by garbage.

item.title: The One Thing Trump Wanted That Pam Bondi Failed to Deliver
item.description: The core of Mr. Trump’s dissatisfaction with the attorney general was apparently her failure to serve his need for revenge against his enemies.

item.title: Trump Has Lost Control of the Iran War
item.description: The pain inflicted on Iran has not forced it into submission. It is not clear what will.

item.title: Is It Legal to Bully the Supreme Court?
item.description: It may be a new world, but “it’s the same Constitution.”

item.title: How Giorgia Meloni Fell to Earth
item.description: For more than three years, Giorgia Meloni’s leadership of Italy has seemed unshakable. Now she suddenly seems vulnerable.

item.title: The Epstein Emails Show #MeToo Never Stood a Chance
item.description: The files reveal the mechanics of doubt that the #MeToo movement had to contend with.

item.title: The Rise and Fall of Pam Bondi
item.description: Readers react to President Trump’s firing of the attorney general. Also: A.I. and therapy.

item.title: Trump Has No Idea How to Clean Up His Own Mess
item.description: His speech told us very little, at least explicitly, but revealed quite a lot.

item.title: Why Does Gavin Newsom Sound Like a Right-Wing Troll?
item.description: Gavin Newsom seems to have concluded that progressive models of manliness are passé.

item.title: Why Iran Believes It Has the Upper Hand
item.description: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains how Iran views the war, and why the United States is losing.

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead?
item.description: A case of mistaken identity, or a profound experience of grief? The religious scholar Bart Ehrman argues that the earliest accounts of Jesus’ Resurrection might be rooted in the way we process loss.

item.title: How Did Trump’s Iran Speech Fare?
item.description: Readers respond to President Trump’s speech on the war. Also: Asian Americans at risk; civic eduction for the 250th; a veteran’s view.

item.title: How Jesus Altered the World’s Conscience
item.description: It might sound obvious now, but helping your neighbor wasn’t always seen as a moral imperative. The New Testament scholar Bart Ehrman explains how Jesus’ teachings shape the way we all view our role in society today.

item.title: Eloquence Has Grown Passé. Let’s Bring It Back Into Fashion.
item.description: Being authentic or relatable shouldn’t mean being inarticulate.

item.title: On Iran, Trump Offers No Plan and No End in Sight
item.description: The president failed to provide any meaningful strategy on the two central issues that define the war on Iran: reopening the Strait of Hormuz and what to do about that country’s uranium.

item.title: Even if This Is the Right War, We’ve Got the Wrong President
item.description: Tastelessness and classlessness are the least of our concerns.

item.title: Trump’s Positive Thinking Won’t End This War
item.description: President Trump’s Wednesday night address about the war in Iran is evidence of how poorly his war of choice is going, the columnist Jamelle Bouie argues.

item.title: What Do You Do When a Family Member Commits a Terrible Crime?
item.description: The columnist M. Gessen sits down with the writer Harriet Clark to talk through the complexity of maintaining relationships with family members who have done unthinkable things.
